item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a totally valid critique, thanks for sharing. This is my first "successful" SCP and my main goal was to capture the spirit of classic SCPs that I love with my own original take on an anomaly. I agree that it's not particularly original, but that's sort of a consequence of what I was aiming for.

I agree that it could be more fleshed out though. I'm hoping to bring more originality and depth to my future articles! Thanks so much for reading and sharing your opinion!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a no-vote for now. This feels like a generic thing that traps you forever, and produces weird media as well to me, with a somewhat thin veneer of film festival put over it. I think if you intensified the effect or did more with the horror of being trapped in it, it would work better. Right now, there isn't a lot that makes it stand out from similar SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The ambiguity of the ending worked beautifully, in my opinion.

The defacing of the task force was unexpected and a nicely understated bit of body horror.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for reading and enjoying it!

I can totally see what you're saying about the ending. Originally I did have Dr. Sydney succumbing to a cognitohazard, but I got notes in the IRC chat that it was a bit unsatisfying and took away her agency. I ended up going in the complete opposite direction, but maybe I should have kept it a bit more open-ended as to whether or not she acted on her own or was compelled to do what she did. I could edit the final note to reflect this if you think it would help the article.

Edit: I went ahead and made the ending a bit more ambiguous. I think it works better, thanks for the note!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was a great read! Although I could easily see the SCP-3008 inspirations coming from it, it managed to have it's own consistent tone and be original enough that it isn't a carbon copy. One thing I don't really like is how it looked like Dr. Sydney was not under the effect of a cognitohazard. I feel as it removes some of the dangerous threat from SCP-5911 but that's a very small complain for an otherwise solid SCP.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks Grigori! I really appreciated all of your notes, they really helped me hone in on what I was trying to do.

So happy you like the ending! I'm really happy with how this ended up turning out and won't take it too personally if it doesn't make the cut.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like how this turned out and think tonally its more consistent now. Like the ending a lot more this way too.

+1

(if for some reason this doesn't survive, I would not give up as i think this concept is quite good)

item.title: The SCP Independent Film Festival
item.content:encoded:
Image Credit: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Carmike_10_Cinema-_Manitowoc,_WI_-_Flickr_-_MichaelSteeber.jpg

Thanks to everyone in the IRC and the forums who gave me crit and guidance on this project. I'm really happy with how it turned out and hope you all enjoy it!
